name: things
title: Things
description: Search and manage your Things to-dos from Raycast
author: loris

commands.name: show-today-list
commands.title: Show Today List
commands.subtitle: Things
commands.description: Search and manage your today to-dos

commands.name: show-inbox-list
commands.title: Show Inbox List
commands.subtitle: Things
commands.description: Search and manage your inbox to-dos

commands.name: show-anytime-list
commands.title: Show Anytime List
commands.subtitle: Things
commands.description: Search and manage your anytime to-dos

commands.name: show-upcoming-list
commands.title: Show Upcoming List
commands.subtitle: Things
commands.description: Search and manage your upcoming to-dos

commands.name: show-someday-list
commands.title: Show Someday List
commands.subtitle: Things
commands.description: Search and manage your someday to-dos

commands.name: quick-find
commands.title: Quick Find
commands.subtitle: Things
commands.description: Search across all areas, projects, and to-dos.

commands.name: show-today-in-menu-bar
commands.title: Show Today in Menu Bar
commands.subtitle: Things
commands.description: See your Today list in MacOS menu-bar

preferences.name: displayTodo
preferences.title: Count
preferences.label: Display To-Do
preferences.description: When enabled, the first incomplete Things to-do will be displayed in the menu bar.

preferences.name: shouldShowShortcuts
preferences.label: Show Lists Shortcuts
preferences.description: When enabled, lists shortcuts (e.g today, upcoming, projects, areas, etc.) will be included in the menu bar.

commands.name: quick-add-todo
commands.title: Quick Add To-Do
commands.subtitle: Things
commands.description: Quickly add a new to-do in Things.

preferences.name: shouldCloseMainWindow
preferences.title: Advanced
preferences.label: Close window immediately
preferences.description: When enabled, the Raycast window is closed immediately, allowing you to carry on with other work.

preferences.name: dontUseAI
preferences.label: Don't use AI
preferences.description: When enabled, the to-do is simply added to your inbox with the input as the title.

tools.name: get-todos
tools.title: Get To-Dos
tools.description: Get to-dos from Things, optionally filtered by list (inbox/today/anytime/upcoming/someday/logbook/trash), project ID, or area ID. inbox/today/anytime/upcoming/someday return open to-dos; logbook returns completed and canceled to-dos; trash returns all trashed to-dos. WARNING: omitting all filters returns ALL open to-dos — only do this when truly needed.

tools.name: get-todo-details
tools.title: Get To-Do Details
tools.description: Get full details of a single to-do including status, notes, tags, and checklist items. Note: checklist items are only returned when the unofficial SQL API is enabled in preferences.

tools.name: get-todos-details
tools.title: Get To-Dos Details
tools.description: Get full details (status, notes, tags, checklist items) for multiple to-dos at once (comma-separated IDs). Always prefer this over calling get-todo-details in a loop. Note: checklist items are only returned when the unofficial SQL API is enabled in preferences.

tools.name: search-todos
tools.title: Search To-Dos
tools.description: Search for open to-dos by keyword (matches title and notes). Use this as the FIRST step when looking for a to-do by name.

tools.name: get-projects
tools.title: Get Projects
tools.description: Get all open projects in Things. Each project includes its id, name, notes, tags, due date, area, and its nested list of open to-dos. Use get-project-details for the open to-do count.

tools.name: get-project-details
tools.title: Get Project Details
tools.description: Get full details of a single project including status, notes, tags, due date, activation date, area, and open to-do count (completed and canceled to-dos are excluded from the count).

tools.name: get-areas
tools.title: Get Areas
tools.description: Get all areas in Things. Each area includes its id, name, tags, and its nested list of open to-dos that are not inside a project.

tools.name: get-area-details
tools.title: Get Area Details
tools.description: Get full details of a single area including tags, open project count, and open to-do count. The to-do count only includes to-dos directly in the area, not to-dos inside a project within the area.

tools.name: get-lists
tools.title: Get Lists
tools.description: Get all user-created lists (open projects and areas) in Things as a flat array ordered so each area is followed by its projects. Each item has id, name, and type ('area' or 'project').

tools.name: add-todo
tools.title: Add To-Do
tools.description: Add a new to-do in Things. No auth token required. Key params: title, notes, when (today/tomorrow/evening/anytime/someday/YYYY-MM-DD), deadline (YYYY-MM-DD), tags (comma-separated), list-id (project/area ID), checklist-items (newline-separated). Use add-json to add a to-do with headings or nested structure.

tools.name: add-project
tools.title: Add Project
tools.description: Add a new project in Things. No auth token required. Key params: title, notes, when (today/tomorrow/evening/anytime/someday/YYYY-MM-DD), deadline (YYYY-MM-DD), tags (comma-separated), area-id, to-dos (newline-separated to-do titles to add immediately). Use add-json to create a project with headings and nested to-dos in one call.

tools.name: add-json
tools.title: Add Via JSON
tools.description: Add items to Things using the JSON URL scheme. Requires an auth token in preferences. This is the ONLY tool that supports creating projects with headings and nested to-dos in a single call. Tags must be arrays of strings, NOT comma-separated strings.

tools.name: update-todo
tools.title: Update To-Do
tools.description: Update multiple fields of a to-do at once. Requires an auth token in preferences. Key params: title, notes, prepend-notes, append-notes, when (today/tomorrow/evening/anytime/someday/YYYY-MM-DD), deadline (YYYY-MM-DD), tags (replaces all), add-tags (adds without replacing), completed (boolean), canceled (boolean), checklist-items, list-id.

tools.name: update-project
tools.title: Update Project
tools.description: Update a project in Things. Requires an auth token in preferences. Key params: title, notes, prepend-notes, append-notes, when, deadline (YYYY-MM-DD), tags (replaces all), add-tags (adds without replacing), area-id (move to a different area), completed (boolean), canceled (boolean).

tools.name: set-todo-property
tools.title: Set To-Do Property
tools.description: Set a single property of a to-do via JXA. Does NOT require an auth token. Allowed keys: name, notes, tagNames, dueDate, activationDate, completionDate, cancellationDate. Date values must be YYYY-MM-DD strings. To mark a to-do complete or canceled, use update-todo with completed/canceled instead of setting status here.

tools.name: delete-todo
tools.title: Delete To-Do
tools.description: Delete a to-do in Things. No auth token required. Prompts the user for confirmation before deleting.

tools.name: delete-project
tools.title: Delete Project
tools.description: Delete a project in Things. No auth token required. Prompts the user for confirmation before deleting. WARNING: deleting a project also moves all its to-dos to the trash.

ai.instructions:
You are a Things task manager assistant.

SEARCH FIRST: When the user refers to a specific to-do or project BY NAME, call search-todos first to find its ID — do not fetch entire lists to find one item. Exception: if the user says 'all to-dos in [list]' or wants to operate on every item in a list, use get-todos with listName instead.

AVOID OVER-FETCHING: Use the most specific filter available (listName, projectId, or areaId). Never fetch all to-dos when a filtered query will do.

BATCH DETAILS: When you need full details (notes, tags, checklist) for multiple to-dos, call get-todos-details once with a comma-separated ID list — never loop over get-todo-details.

WRITE OPERATIONS: Use update-todo for status changes (completed/canceled) and multi-field updates. Use set-todo-property only for a single scalar field like dueDate, activationDate, name, or notes. add-todo, add-project, set-todo-property, delete-todo, and delete-project do NOT require an auth token. update-todo, update-project, and add-json DO require an auth token in preferences.

LISTS: Built-in lists (inbox, today, anytime, upcoming, someday) are always available — never call get-lists to check if they exist. Move a to-do between built-in lists by setting 'when' (today, tomorrow, evening, anytime, someday, or YYYY-MM-DD). Use 'list-id' only to move into a user project or area (get the ID from get-projects or get-areas).

DATES: Dates are YYYY-MM-DD. When dueDateIsRecurring is true, the due date shifts each time the task repeats — do NOT treat it as a fixed deadline. IMPORTANT: dueDateIsRecurring and isRecurring are only populated with real values when the user has enabled the 'Read directly from Things database' preference (useUnofficialApi). On the default JXA path both fields are always false — even for tasks that are genuinely recurring — because Apple Events do not expose recurrence rule internals. When dueDateIsRecurring is false and isRecurring is false, you cannot determine from the data alone whether a task is recurring; treat due dates as fixed deadlines and avoid asserting that a task is non-recurring.

CREATING WITH STRUCTURE: Use add-json to create a project with headings and nested to-dos in one call. Tags in JSON must be arrays (e.g. ["work", "urgent"]), not comma-separated strings.

evals.input: @things what are my to-dos for today?
@things mark "buy milk" in inbox as done
@things set the deadline for "buy milk" in inbox to saturday
@things move all to-dos from my inbox to today

preferences.name: authToken
preferences.title: Authentication Token
preferences.description: You can find your unique token in Things' settings. go to Things → Settings → General → Enable Things URLs → Manage

preferences.name: thingsAppIdentifier
preferences.title: Things version switcher
preferences.description: Switch between Things or Things Beta

preferences.name: groupByProjectOrArea
preferences.title: Display
preferences.label: Group by Project or Area
preferences.description: Group to-dos in lists by their project or area

preferences.name: useUnofficialApi
preferences.title: Unofficial API
preferences.label: Read directly from Things database
preferences.description: Requires Full Disk Access for Raycast and uses an unofficial internal format that may break on Things updates.
